---
title: "LRP69: Does baseline non-verbal ability predict verbal/reading trajectory shape? (joint growth curves, independent core)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP69 answers issue #187 (Q5): it characterises the **longitudinal trajectories**
of the verbal and reading measures across the four RLI waves and asks whether a
child's **baseline non-verbal ability** predicts the *shape* of those trajectories
— principally the growth **rate**.

It is a joint multivariate **latent growth-curve** model: each measure has its own
per-child latent intercept and linear age slope on the logit scale, and baseline
non-verbal ability (`blocks`) enters as a per-child predictor of both. This is the
*descriptive*, growth-shape complement to LRP67 (which models what within-child
*change* drives reading change). LRP69 is the **independent-core** model (no
cross-measure coupling); LRP70 adds a shared growth-tempo factor.

::: {.callout-note title="Symbol mapping (read before the tables)"}

| Construct | Symbol | Measure | Denominator |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Receptive vocabulary | **R** | `rowpvt` | 170 |
| Expressive vocabulary | **E** | `eowpvt` | 170 |
| Receptive grammar | **T** | `trog` | 32 |
| Word reading | **W** | `ewrswr` | 79 |
| Letter-sound knowledge | **L** | `yarclet` | 32 |

Non-verbal ability is **block design** (`blocks`, WPPSI-III Block Design),
recorded at t1 only and complete for all 54 children — a time-invariant baseline
predictor, not a trajectory outcome.
:::

## Model structure

For each measure $m \in \{R, E, T, W, L\}$, child $i$ and wave $t$, a latent logit
level follows a linear growth curve in standardised age $a$:

$$
\theta_m[i,t] = \text{intercept}_m[i] + \text{slope}_m[i]\,a[i,t],
$$

with baseline non-verbal ability $z(\text{blocks}_i)$ entering both:

$$
\text{intercept}_m[i] = \alpha_m + \delta_m\,z(\text{blocks}_i) + \sigma^0_m z^0_m[i],
\qquad
\text{slope}_m[i] = \beta_m + \gamma_m\,z(\text{blocks}_i) + \sigma^1_m z^1_m[i].
$$

$\gamma_m$ (baseline non-verbal ability → growth **rate**) is the headline Q5
estimand; $\delta_m$ is the effect on the **level at the sample-mean (mid-study)
age** — age is standardised over all child-wave cells, so the intercept is at the
pooled mean age, not at study entry (the entry-level association is
$\delta_m + \gamma_m\,\mathbb{E}[\text{age\_std at } t_1]$). Observed counts enter
a **masked** Beta-Binomial ($\kappa_m$ dispersion) so a child missing one score
still contributes its other waves. The child random intercept and slope are
**independent per measure** — the within-measure intercept–slope correlation is
deliberately omitted at n≈54, mirroring the joint ITT model's disabled LKJ residual
correlation. The intervention-dose covariate is omitted (the revised DAG's `IS`
collider). Everything is non-centred.

{{< include _partials/_results_growth.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
